description: Illustrative token-cost scenario for one agent 'social cycle'. This is not a measured Moltbook trace. Assumptions are explicit so the arithmetic is auditable.

sources.label: OpenClaw Context docs
sources.notes: Used to anchor the claim that context overhead can already exceed ~14k session tokens in a small documented example. It also provides the ~9,603 system prompt example.

sources.label: Anthropic, Claude Opus 4.6
sources.notes: Used for the official $5 / $25 per million token pricing.

sources.label: Anthropic, Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.6
sources.notes: Used for the official $3 / $15 per million token pricing.

sources.label: Artificial Analysis, MiniMax-M2.5
sources.notes: Used for current MiniMax-M2.5 token pricing.

context_components_per_action.name: System prompt
context_components_per_action.basis: Rounded from the OpenClaw docs example (~9,603 tokens).

context_components_per_action.name: AGENTS.md and project instructions
context_components_per_action.basis: Assumption for a richer project than the docs example.

context_components_per_action.name: Tool descriptions and schemas
context_components_per_action.basis: Assumption representing a trimmed but still tool-heavy agent configuration.

context_components_per_action.name: Agent persona and state
context_components_per_action.basis: Assumption for persona, goals, and current state.

context_components_per_action.name: Prior history
context_components_per_action.basis: Assumption for a non-trivial conversation or feed context.

notes: This scenario is intentionally labeled as illustrative rather than observed.
The main point is that repeated context loading dominates cost under current architectures.
